# Planning Brief - 2026-06-28

Source: `.github/research/2026-06-28-research.md`

Open `auto-implement` queue before this run: **0** (verified via
`gh issue list --label auto-implement --state open`). Prior brief's
three items (#862, #863, #864) are all closed, so the research's
"drain prior brief first" suggestion is already satisfied — this run
proceeds directly with the three new items.

## Summary

Three small, self-contained items derived from upstream commits on
2026-06-27. All three are independent at the file level. Execution
order recommended by the research brief: #1 (provider error cause,
~2h, isolated) -> #2 (per-routed-model usage, ~3h, UI only) -> #3
(compaction token budgeting, ~3h, hot path). Total ~8h, below the
5-issue cap but the research brief produced exactly three actionable
items and inflating the batch would dilute backlog quality.

Items explicitly **not** turned into issues:

- claude-code (8 cycles dry) and aider (31 days silence): cadence
  problem, not a code item. Belongs to `role-management` patching
  `agent1-research-system.md` to retag both repos `<!-- monthly
  (1st Mon) -->`. No `auto-implement` issue.
- opencode #34171 (V2Event SSE) and #34167 (empty allowlist):
  sst-effect internals, no analogue in our stack.

the implementation tasks per issue for the engineering pipeline.

## Codebase validation notes

The research brief's line numbers were verified against HEAD:

- `src/core/orchestrator.ts:84` -- `throw err;` inside the
  `provider.complete` catch block. Confirmed; no `formatProviderError`
  exists yet. `src/providers/format.ts` is absent.
- `src/core/streamingOrchestrator.ts:162` -- second rethrow site,
  same shape; must also be wrapped to stay consistent. The research
  brief mentions only the non-streaming site but the streaming path
  shares the same UX problem.
- `src/providers/auth.ts:6-15` -- `AuthError(message, provider, cause)`
  already stores `cause`. Cause-preservation is purely a display fix.
- `src/cli/interactive.ts:1298` -- `today` branch lacks a `By Model:`
  block; `:1329` (`month`) and `:1355` (`all`) both have one. Pattern
  is trivially copyable.
- `src/cli/tui/components/StatusBar.tsx:8-18` -- `StatusBarProps`
  interface; extending with `topModelLabel?: string` is additive.
  Render slot is line 100-114 (the cost/tokens segment).
- `src/cli/tui/pages/ChatPage.tsx:103` and
  `src/cli/tui/pages/LogsPage.tsx:90` -- the only two `<StatusBar />`
  call sites that already feed `cost` from `getCostTracker()`. Adding
  `topModelLabel` requires no new poll loop.
- `src/core/costTracker.ts:59-62` -- `byModel` field already on
  `CostSummary`. `:374-399` populates it from records, `:428`
  (`getTodaySummary`) and `:444` (`getMonthSummary`) both return it.
- `src/core/compaction.ts:71` -- `estimateMessagesTokens` ignores
  `Message.tokens`. `:91` -- `shouldCompact` compares chars/4 against
  raw `maxContextTokens * threshold/100` with no output reserve.
  `:262` -- `compactConversation` slices by `preserveLastN` only,
  never targets a sub-budget.
- `src/core/sessionManager.ts:27` -- `Message.tokens?: { input?:
  number; output?: number }` is declared; orchestrator
  (`:93-98`) records it on every turn. Already wired upstream of
  compaction.

## Risk and ordering

- **Issue 1 (provider error cause)**: lowest risk. New file +
  one-line message rewrite at two rethrow sites. Existing
  `instanceof` checks survive because we mutate `.message` rather
  than wrapping. The only subtle bit is depth-bound on recursion
  (depth 2) to keep diff minimal and prevent runaway chains.
- **Issue 2 (per-model usage)**: UI only. The risk is regressing
  existing `/cost month` / `/cost all` rendering; mitigation is to
  copy the exact block instead of refactoring all three. StatusBar
  prop is additive (optional with default `undefined`), so existing
  tests in `tests/cli/tui/StatusBar.test.tsx` keep passing.
- **Issue 3 (compaction)**: changes core behaviour of an already-
  shipped function. Risk is breaking the existing test suite in
  `src/core/__tests__/compaction.test.ts`. Mitigation: accept the
  old `shouldCompact(messages, maxContextTokens, threshold)`
  positional signature (now also `(messages, max, opts)`), and only
  apply 80%-target logic when the caller passes the new options
  bag. Existing callers (only `SessionManager.checkAndCompact`)
  remain on the legacy path; a follow-up issue wires the bag.

## Deferred / not in this batch

- opencode #34171 (V2Event SSE) -- no effect-schema analogue.
- opencode #34167 (empty allowlist) -- `src/core/router.ts` already
  treats absent routes as match-any per the research note.
- claude-code + aider cadence demotion -- not an `auto-implement`
  code change. Tracked for `role-management`.
- Wiring `reserveOutputTokens` through `SessionManager.checkAndCompact`
  -- intentional follow-up so issue 3 stays small and reviewable.
